reasoning: Query asks specifically about Tajima beef; response leads with MAFF GI #2 (但馬牛) and immediately follows with Kobe Beef (#3), correctly showing the origin relationship. Authoritative official data. One caveat: 195 items returned when only beef was queried — no keyword filter applied, so response includes unrelated Hyogo foods like Sayo Mochi Daizu. Rule B: single dim hit on practical_usefulness (noise from bulk dump).
missing_data: No filter narrowing to beef; agent must sift through 195 items to find the relevant ones. Specific restaurants or butcher shops where Tajima beef can be eaten are absent.
improvement_hint: Support keyword arg in get_local_food to filter by name/category; e.g. get_local_food({prefecture:'Hyogo', q:'但馬牛', lang:'en'}) would surface the two beef entries first.

reasoning: Arabic query answered in Arabic (lang:'ar' honored, Rule D not triggered). MAFF GI #2 但馬牛 and #3 神戸ビーフ both appear with Arabic descriptions at high confidence. Same bulk-dump caveat as L1-01 (195 items) applies, slightly reducing practical_usefulness.
missing_data: Same as L1-01: keyword filter missing; 195 items with most unrelated to beef. No visit/purchasing guidance.
improvement_hint: Same as L1-01: keyword filtering in get_local_food would let Arabic-speaking users get a focused beef-specific answer.

reasoning: Query explicitly asks about Minami-Yamashiro village tea fields, NOT Kyoto city landmarks. Tool returned get_japan_heritage(prefecture:Kyoto) with top_canonical_heritage showing Nijo Castle, To-ji, Nanzen-ji, Nishi Honganji — none related to Minami-Yamashiro tea. The response entirely misses the village-level intent. Rule A: score data returned, not the tool mismatch — but data is wholly irrelevant to the actual query intent. Rule C does not help since the relevant entity class (Yamashiro tea village) is completely absent.
missing_data: Any data on Minami-Yamashiro village (南山城村), its tea gardens, tea GI designation (南山城村茶), or access. Wikidata entity Q for the village itself is absent.
improvement_hint: Route village-specific queries through get_spots(municipality:'南山城村') or search_area(q:'南山城村 茶') rather than prefecture-level heritage; add municipality param to get_japan_heritage.

reasoning: Spanish query answered in Spanish (lang:'es' honored). Top item is 吉田の火祭 (Yoshida Fire Festival) designated as Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property — correct hit. One caveat: description is minimal (only city-level), no date field present (null), and the national_heritage section appends unrelated UNESCO items (Dainichido Bugaku, Kawagoe etc.) creating noise. Rule B: scope gap on specificity/practical_usefulness. Second item (hanabi fireworks) is irrelevant noise.
missing_data: Festival date (usually late August), specific location at Kitaguchi Sengen Shrine (北口本宮冨士浅間神社), visual description of fire ritual, access from nearest station.
improvement_hint: Populate date field for bunka_intangible events from Wikidata P585 where available; filter national_heritage noise when local result already matched the query.

reasoning: Query asks specifically about Nakagei region yuzu (中芸ゆず). Response returns all 651 Kochi foods, truncated at 500. Top item is 物部ゆず (different yuzu, Kami City, not Nakagei). The Nakagei GI (中芸地区ゆず) is absent or buried. Items 2-onwards are unrelated sightseeing pages — badly off-topic. Rule C does not apply: the specific GI entity class is missing or inaccessible.
missing_data: MAFF GI record for 中芸地区ゆず (registration #97 or similar) covering Nahari/Umaji/Kitagawa/Yasuda/Toyo towns; yuzu processing villages; access info.
improvement_hint: Support q= keyword filtering in get_local_food to surface the exact GI match; also fix scraper to exclude non-food sightseeing pages from food category results.

reasoning: Query in Chinese (zh) about Hirosaki Park cherry blossoms; tool returned get_spots in English (no lang param passed). Rule D: lang mismatch — Chinese query, English+Japanese response — constraint_handling drops by 1. Response correctly surfaces 弘前公園 (Hirosaki Park, sakura_meisho_100 kind) and 弘前城 with coordinates and website. name_zh fields present for key items. Practical_usefulness is good but bloom dates absent.
missing_data: Cherry blossom bloom timing (late April), festival dates, entry fees during festival period, access from Hirosaki Station.
improvement_hint: Pass lang:'zh' to get_spots when query language is Chinese; add bloom_season metadata to sakura_meisho_100 items.

reasoning: Korean query about Izumo Taisha; tool called with lang:'ko' but search_area returns results in English (name_en fields), not Korean. Rule D: constraint_handling -1 from otherwise 4. Top result is Q696362 出雲大社 (Izumo Taisha) with National Treasure designation, coordinates, website, opening hours — exactly what was asked. 88 match_count includes station, precinct site, Honden, providing context. Practical_usefulness: visit logistics present but Korean descriptions absent.
missing_data: Korean-language descriptions (name_ko, description_ko); annual Kamiari-zuki significance; marriage/en-musubi context relevant to Korean travelers.
improvement_hint: Ensure search_area returns name_ko and translated description in Korean when lang='ko'; currently only English fields are populated in results.

reasoning: French query about Itsukushima Shrine; tool passed lang:'en' (not 'fr'). Rule D: French not served, constraint_handling -1. Response is factually excellent: Q191763 leads with Itsukushima Shrine (UNESCO WHS, National Treasure), 105 matches. Good coordinates, website. No French translations in output. Also includes island entity Q875301 and sub-components. One caveat: no ferry/access info to Miyajima Island.
missing_data: French-language descriptions; Miyajima Island ferry access from Hiroshima/Miyajimaguchi; deer and torii tidal schedule note.
improvement_hint: Pass lang:'fr' to search_area so French translations in dataset (if available) are returned; add access_note field for island/ferry-dependent attractions.

reasoning: German query about Kumano Kodo; tool called get_japan_heritage(prefecture:Wakayama, lang:'en') — not German (Rule D: constraint -1). The tool_scope_note itself warns: 'pilgrimage routes (Kumano Kodo) — see see_also_wikidata_heritage OR call search_area'. Response surfaces Kumano Kodo (Q1148078) in top_canonical_heritage with UNESCO WHS designation, and Kumano Sanzan. However the Japan Heritage items are unrelated Wakayama stories. Rule C: relevant entity partially present in top_canonical_heritage (score 3), but the primary delivery channel (Japan Heritage stories) misses it. Better tool would be search_area.
missing_data: German-language descriptions; specific route options (Nakahechi, Iseji, Kohechi); trailhead access; recommended walking sections; accommodation along route.
improvement_hint: For Kumano Kodo queries, route to search_area(q:'熊野古道', lang:'de') which surfaces the heritage entity directly; get_japan_heritage is the wrong tool for pilgrimage route queries.

reasoning: Russian query about Himeji Castle; tool passed lang:'en'. Rule D: Russian not served, constraint_handling -1. Response excellent: Q188754 leads with Himeji Castle (UNESCO WHS, National Treasure, Special Historic Site), full opening hours, phone, website, wheelchair:no info. 66 matches include multiple turret/keep components (Ha Watariyagura, Main Keep, Northwest/East Small Keeps) all with heritage designations.
missing_data: Russian-language text; admission price details; white heron castle seasonal context; nearby garden Koko-en.
improvement_hint: Pass lang:'ru' when Russian query detected; add price_yen field to complement price_band for high-traffic heritage sites.

reasoning: Thai query about Bunraku in Osaka; lang:'th' honored. Top result is Q243170 文楽 (Bunraku) UNESCO ICH 2003, Thai name บุนราคุ, with Thai description. Factually correct. Caveat: no Osaka-specific constraint honored — tool returned all 183 traditional arts nationwide with no geographic filter. Practical_usefulness limited since agent must identify Osaka venue (National Bunraku Theatre) independently from this bulk list. Rule B: single hit on constraint_handling/specificity for geographic scope gap.
missing_data: National Bunraku Theatre (国立文楽劇場) Osaka location, performance schedule, ticket purchase link; performance seasons.
improvement_hint: Add prefecture or city filter to get_traditional_arts; link UNESCO ICH entries to associated performance venues via wikidata_venues see_also block.

reasoning: Vietnamese query about Yumihama Kasuri (弓浜絣), a traditional cotton kasuri textile. Tool returned 7 items — all MAFF GI food entries (rakkyo, broccoli, persimmon, watermelon). 弓浜絣 is a METI craft (textile), not food. The craft entry is entirely absent. Rule A: data returned is authoritative food GIs but wholly off-intent for a textile query.
missing_data: METI traditional craft entry for 弓浜絣 (Yumihama Kasuri cotton ikat textile from Tottori); weaving history, production area (Saihaku-gun), where to see demonstrations.
improvement_hint: get_local_specialty should return craft items alongside food by default when no category filter is passed; verify 弓浜絣 is present in the Tottori craft records and ranked near top for Tottori queries.

reasoning: Filipino query about Naoshima Art Island; tool returned lang:'en' (not Filipino/Tagalog). Rule D: Tagalog not served, constraint_handling -1. Response good: Benesse Art Site Naoshima (Q11337011) and Naoshima New Museum of Art both present. Municipality entity, scrape spots including Chinese-language tourism association pages also present — some noise. Access info (ferry to island) absent. Coordinates missing for Benesse Art Site (null). One factual caveat: 'Naoshima New Museum' opened 2026 — possible newer addition, data may be current.
missing_data: Ferry access from Takamatsu/Uno; Chichu Art Museum, Yayoi Kusama pumpkin sculpture; opening hours for key museums; island bike rental info.

reasoning: English query about Kakunodate samurai district; get_spots(prefecture:Akita, municipality:角館) returns excellent result. Top item Q11428562 角館武家屋敷 with highly enriched description_en mentioning preservation district, cherry blossoms, samurai residences. Weeping cherry trees entry (Q111721103) with Natural Monument designation. Cherry Bark Work Museum also present. All dims close to 5; minor caveat: no specific individual residence names (Ishiguro-ke, Aoyagi-ke).
missing_data: Individual samurai residence names open to public (石黒家, 青柳家); admission fees; cherry blossom timing (late April); distance from Shinkansen stop at Kakunodate Station.
improvement_hint: Enrich description_en with names of the six publicly accessible samurai residences; add nearby_station field for walkability context.

reasoning: Indonesian query about Onomichi; tool returned no lang param (default English). Rule D: Indonesian not served, constraint_handling -1. Response has Shimanami Kaido (Q11514108, cycling route), Onomichi City Museum of Art, Onomichi city entity with Japan Heritage designation. Relevant and factually sound. Caveat: Onomichi's iconic temple walk (千光寺, 艮神社), cat alley (猫の細道), and literary connections not surfaced. Specificity limited.
missing_data: Senkoji Temple (千光寺), cat alley, Onomichi's literary heritage (Naoki Sanjugo etc.), ropeway to Senkoji Hill; Indonesian language descriptions.
improvement_hint: Pass lang:'id' for Indonesian queries; boost municipality-specific spots for Onomichi including temple_walk kind entries.

reasoning: Spanish query about Nachi Falls; tool passed lang:'en'. Rule D: Spanish not served, constraint_handling -1. Response leads with Q1365882 那智滝 (Nachi Falls, waterfall) with Natural Monument + Scenic Beauty designations, coordinates, sound-scape listing. Also includes 那智原始林 (Primeval Forest), 那智四十八滝 (48 waterfalls), temple components. Good specificity. Caveat: no UNESCO WHS mention in Nachi Falls entry itself (it's part of Sacred Sites Kii Mountain Range WHS). No website/visiting hours for the falls.
missing_data: Spanish descriptions; UNESCO WHS affiliation of Nachi area; Nachi Taisha shrine relationship; seasonal waterfall viewing conditions; access from Nachi Station.

reasoning: Hindi query about Shiretoko Peninsula; tool passed lang:'en'. Rule D: Hindi not served, constraint_handling -1. Response excellent: Q629238 知床半島 (Shiretoko Peninsula, UNESCO WHS), Q739391 知床国立公園 (Shiretoko National Park), Q17208861 知床 (UNESCO WHS), Q11584569 World Heritage Conservation Center with website. 90 matches. Minor note: travel_feasibility -1 for remoteness not flagged (Shiretoko is remote, requires specific access planning from Shari or Utoro).
missing_data: Hindi descriptions; access route (JR to Shiretoko Shari + bus); best season (June-October); drift ice season (Feb) note; wildlife (brown bear, Blakiston's fish owl) highlights.

reasoning: English query about Dewa Sanzan in Yamagata; search_area(q:'出羽', lang:'en'). Top hit is Q135194465 出羽神社三神合祭殿 (low prominence 0.65) then Q1155726 出羽三山 (Three Mountains of Dewa, prominence 0.9, Japan Heritage, pilgrimage_site kinds). However the top result is the less-important shrine hall rather than Dewa Sanzan itself. Subsequent items (出羽金沢城跡 in Akita, 出羽桜美術館 in Yamagata, 出羽山地 mountain range) dilute relevance. Rule B: ranking failure — Dewa Sanzan (Q1155726) should be ranked #1. Practical_usefulness affected by ordering.
missing_data: Individual mountain details (Haguro-san, Gas-san, Yudono-san); yamabushi ascetic training context; seasonal access (Gas-san snow until July); access from Tsurugaoka.
improvement_hint: Boost prominence_score for Q1155726 出羽三山 (currently 0.9) above sub-shrine entries; consider Yamagata-specific search to avoid Akita noise from '出羽' matches.

reasoning: Portuguese query about Yakushima; tool passed lang:'en'. Rule D: Portuguese not served, constraint_handling -1. Response excellent: Q242176 屋久島 leads (UNESCO WHS, Wilderness Area, Ramsar, prominence 1.0), followed by 屋久島スギ原始林 (primeval cedar forest), 屋久島国立公園. 97 matches. Minor travel_feasibility caveat: no access info (ferry from Kagoshima, 2-4h; flight option).
missing_data: Portuguese descriptions; Jomon Sugi (縄文杉) trekking info; Wilson Stump; access from Kagoshima (ferry/jet foil); permit system for trails; accommodation types (camping vs lodge).

reasoning: Korean query about Sado Island broadly; tool returned get_japan_heritage(Niigata, ko). top_canonical_heritage surfaces 佐渡金山 (UNESCO WHS) correctly with Korean translations. Japan Heritage items (Shinano River pottery story) are unrelated to Sado. Response covers only the mine; island culture (Noh, Kodo drumming, toki ibis) entirely absent. Rule C: authoritative data but scope gap.
missing_data: Sado Island overview: Noh theater tradition (佐渡の能), Kodo drumming (鼓童), toki ibis (朱鷺), Ogi traditional boat (たらい舟), island access (ferry from Niigata 2.5h); Korean tourism context.
improvement_hint: For island queries, chain search_area(q:'佐渡島') to get broader spot coverage alongside heritage; enhance 佐渡 entries with performing arts and wildlife kinds.

reasoning: Query asks for traditional onsen ryokan in Tohoku region (6 prefectures: Aomori, Iwate, Miyagi, Yamagata, Fukushima, Akita). Tool only queried Akita (1 prefecture). Response shows valid 日本秘湯を守る会 members in Akita — authoritative hito_yu_kai source. But coverage is 1/6 of Tohoku. Rule C: authoritative data but scope gap (only Akita). Practical_usefulness limited by single-prefecture coverage and missing coordinates for many entries. Travel feasibility cannot be assessed without coordinates.
missing_data: Onsen ryokan from Aomori (Sukayu, Tsuta), Iwate (Genbikei Kanko Hotel), Miyagi (Naruko Onsen), Yamagata (Ginzan Onsen), Fukushima (Higashiyama). No coordinates on most Akita entries.
improvement_hint: For Tohoku region queries, fan out get_hotels across all 6 Tohoku prefectures or implement region-level parameter; populate coordinates from official hito_yu_kai member pages.

reasoning: Query asks for 宿坊 (shukubo — temple lodging) for Shikoku pilgrimage. Tool returned get_hotels(prefecture:Tokushima) with OSM/Wikidata hotels — guest houses, onsen ryokan, generic hotels. No shukubo-type accommodation appears in visible results. Shukubo is a distinct lodging category (staying at Buddhist temples along the 88-temple circuit) and is entirely absent. This is a retrieval failure for the specific lodging type requested. Rule C does not apply as the entity class is wholly absent.
missing_data: Temple lodging (宿坊) at Ryozen-ji (#1 temple), Gokurakuji (#2), and other Tokushima temples along the Ohenro circuit; shukubo booking guidance; ohenro etiquette.
improvement_hint: Add shukubo as a lodging_type in the hotel dataset; scrape 宿坊 listings from temple official pages; add ohenro_temple kind to link accommodation with pilgrimage circuit.

reasoning: Chinese query asks for Hokkaido farm experience (農家体験/agri-tourism). Tool returned get_spots(prefecture:Hokkaido, limit:50) — general tourist attractions (Showa-shinzan volcano, Sapporo Clock Tower, Matsumae Castle). No farm experience venues appear in visible results. Farm stays (農泊) and agri-tourism (体験農場) are a niche category absent from the spot data. Rule A applies: wrong tool/approach for this intent, and returned data is wholly off-topic for farm experience. Score data as returned — some Hokkaido spots are authoritative but irrelevant to query.
missing_data: Farm stay (農泊) facilities; lavender farm experiences in Furano; dairy farm tours in Tokachi; pick-your-own fruit farms; agri-tourism booking info.
improvement_hint: Add agri_tourism and farm_stay kinds to spot taxonomy; scrape JA (農協) farm experience listings and Hokkaido rural tourism pages; implement kind-filter in get_spots.

reasoning: English query about traditional pottery in Kyushu; Saga targeted correctly (Arita/Imari). Returns METI crafts: Hakata Ori (textile — noise, but Rule A: authoritative data), Imari-Arita Ware, Karatsu Ware — both exactly right. English descriptions high confidence. Query says 'Kyushu' broadly but only Saga returned; travel_feasibility caveat: no kiln locations listed.
missing_data: Kiln town maps for Arita and Karatsu; pottery workshop experiences; other Kyushu ceramics (Hasami-yaki in Nagasaki, Satsuma-yaki in Kagoshima); visitor center info for Arita Ceramics Museum.
improvement_hint: For 'Kyushu' ceramic queries, fan out to Nagasaki and Kagoshima too; add kiln_visit and workshop_experience kinds to craft records; link METI craft entries to associated tourist spots.

reasoning: Korean query about Tohoku traditional festivals; tool only queried Aomori prefecture (1/6 of Tohoku). Returns 2 items: 八戸三社大祭 and 青森ねぶた — both valid Aomori festivals with Korean translations (lang:'ko' honored). national_heritage appends off-topic UNESCO items from outside Tohoku. Missing the other 5 Tohoku prefectures' famous festivals (Sendai Tanabata, Akita Kanto, Yamagata Hanagasa, Morioka Sansa, Fukushima Waraji). Rule C: authoritative data with major coverage gap (Aomori only). Specificity at 3 because what is returned is specifically correct.
missing_data: Sendai Tanabata (仙台七夕), Akita Kanto (秋田竿燈), Yamagata Hanagasa (山形花笠), Morioka Sansa Odori (盛岡さんさ踊り), Fukushima Waraji (わらじ祭り) — all major Tohoku festivals absent.
improvement_hint: Implement region-level fanout for get_festivals (e.g. region:'tohoku') to query all 6 Tohoku prefectures simultaneously; filter national_heritage to region-relevant items only.
